mappings.rationaleAI: Core text for this section, covering instinct, fixed action patterns, learning, and the interplay of genes and experience in shaping behaviour (A, B.2, D).
Covers the nervous system's role in receiving sensory information, processing it in the brain and spinal cord, and initiating behavioural responses (C.3).
Directly addresses hormonal influences on behaviour including sex hormones, and the interaction of endocrine and nervous systems (C.1, C.2).
Covers plant movements including tropic responses and other autonomous movements relevant to stereotyped plant responses (B.1).
Directly relevant to photoperiodism and molecular clock mechanisms that govern rhythmic behavioural patterns (B.3).
Covers genetic determination of behaviour through natural selection and the evolutionary basis of behavioural traits (D.1, D.2).
Covers sensory reception and visual processing central to understanding how organisms detect stimuli and initiate behavioural responses (C.3).
Addresses genetic determination of traits including behavioural phenotypes, sex linkage, and variation underlying the evolution of behaviour (D.1).
Illustrates the diversity of mammalian behaviour and the role of brain evolution in shaping complex behavioural repertoires (A.1, D).
Covers conditioning and learning processes that shape behavioural responses, directly relevant to the influence of experience on behaviour (D.2).
Covers nervous system development and the biological foundations underlying the emergence of behavioural responses in organisms (C.3).
Covers insect behavioural responses to environmental stimuli, relevant to taxes, fixed action patterns, and predator-prey interactions (B.2).
